name: Precious Brady-Davis
bio: Precious Brady-Davis is a transgender activist, author, and environmental advocate serving as MWRD Commissioner. She was appointed to the board on July 5, 2023 by Gov. J.B. Pritzker to fill the vacancy left by Kimberly du Buclet — making her the first openly transgender elected official in Illinois. She won an uncontested special Democratic primary on March 19, 2024 (425,160 votes), then defeated Republican R. Cary Capparelli 69.3% to 30.7% (1,284,203 to 567,911 votes) in the November 2024 general election. She is seeking her first full 6-year elected term in 2026. She is the author of 'I Have Always Been Me' (2018 memoir). Her environmental justice platform centers South and West Side communities, green infrastructure, climate resilience, and ending MWRD land leases to polluters.
prior_office: MWRD Commissioner (appointed July 5, 2023 by Gov. Pritzker; special election Nov. 2024–present). Former communications professional and LGBTQ+ rights advocate. Author of 'I Have Always Been Me' (2018 memoir).

red_flags.label: Lost 2022 MWRD primary before being appointed
red_flags.detail: Brady-Davis placed 5th in the June 2022 Democratic primary with 10.9% (126,672 votes), losing to incumbents. She was subsequently appointed by Gov. Pritzker in 2023 after Kimberly du Buclet's vacancy — a path to office via gubernatorial appointment rather than direct voter mandate.

key_votes.summary: Voted to advance the Ostara nutrient recovery program — phosphorus removal from wastewater to protect waterways.
Supported drug take-back partnership with Cook County Sheriff to reduce pharmaceutical pollution in water systems.
Supported expanding green infrastructure in playgrounds — permeable surfaces reducing stormwater overflow.

key_votes.bill: MWRD Land Lease Policy — Polluter Tenants
key_votes.summary: Publicly advocates for evicting MWRD land tenants that pollute waterways. 'Evict toxic tenants: We must stop leasing MWRD land to polluters who endanger our waterways, wildlife, and public health.'
Advocates to complete Stage II of the Deep Tunnel (McCook Reservoir) by 2029 to dramatically increase flood retention capacity.

policy_platform.position: Direct MWRD resources to South and West Sides — neighborhoods hit hardest by flooding but receiving least financial help. Evict toxic tenants from MWRD-owned land.
Build permeable playgrounds and green spaces to prevent flooding; create job training and mentorships in the trades alongside green infrastructure work.
Cut MWRD greenhouse gas emissions by 50%; install new primary treatment systems to convert methane to usable energy; advocate for wind energy to power aeration at MWRD facilities.
Transform rain barrels into public art; complete McCook Reservoir (Deep Tunnel Stage II) by 2029.
Strengthen water quality monitoring; remove invasive species; end MWRD facilitation of methane production.
Expand wetland parks, preserves, and bike trails along Calumet River and other waterways for public nature access.

career_history.org: Metropolitan Water Reclamation District of Greater Chicago
career_history.highlight: Appointed July 5, 2023 by Gov. J.B. Pritzker; won 2024 special general election 69.3% to 30.7%; first openly transgender elected official in Illinois.
15+ years advancing public health and LGBTQ+ rights advocacy in Chicago.
Published 'I Have Always Been Me' — memoir about gender identity and transgender experience.

endorsements: Service Employees International Union (SEIU) Illinois State Council

social_pulse.summary: High-profile national figure due to transgender identity milestone. Strong positive sentiment among progressive, LGBTQ+, and environmental justice communities. Broad media coverage since 2023 appointment.
